rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope only when bindings are implementation-level and refer to subcomponents or connections already defined in the AADL model.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL structure by anchoring AGREE annexes to the exact component or implementation scope that owns the relevant features.
Only reference ports, subcomponents, or connections explicitly declared in the AADL model within the annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for all AGREE contracts, and place implementation-specific bindings inside implementation annexes.
Ensure all variables in AGREE expressions correspond to visible AADL features (ports, data, subcomponents) with matching scope and type.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Avoid introducing subcomponent paths or implementation-specific references in component type annexes.
